name: Suspicious TCP Tunnel Via PowerShell Script
notes:
This rule detects PowerShell scripts that creates sockets/listeners which could be indicative of tunneling activity.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Suspicious TCP Tunnel Via PowerShell Script
SIGMA Description: Detects powershell scripts that creates sockets/listeners which could be indicative of tunneling activity


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution
when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [Net.HttpWebRequest or Net.Sockets.TcpListener or AcceptTcpClient]
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious TCP Tunnel Via PowerShell Script on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [Net.HttpWebRequest or Net.Sockets.TcpListener or AcceptTcpClient]
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious TCP Tunnel Via PowerShell Script
Event description: Detects PowerShell scripts that creates sockets/listeners which could be indicative of tunneling activity
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
